title: iridorkis obcordata
intro: Iridorkis obcordata, also known as the "creeping fox glove," is a type of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and coughs. Iridorkis obcordata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
